name: Safe Plan-Apply Workflow
description: Review Terraform plans carefully and use targeted applies to minimize blast radius when making infrastructure changes.

steps.title: Run terraform plan and save to file
steps.description: Generate an execution plan and save it as a binary file for later review or application.
steps.tip: Always save the plan to a file. This ensures the exact reviewed plan is what gets applied.

steps.title: Review the plan in detail
steps.description: Display the saved plan in human-readable format to review every change before applying.

steps.title: Apply only the saved plan
steps.description: Apply the exact saved plan without re-computing changes, ensuring consistency with what was reviewed.
steps.warning: Never run 'terraform apply' without a saved plan in production. It recomputes changes which may differ from what you reviewed.

steps.title: Target a specific resource for apply
steps.description: Apply changes to only a specific resource when you want to limit the blast radius.
steps.tip: Targeted applies are useful for debugging but should not be the norm. Terraform warns about this for a reason.

steps.title: Use detailed exit codes in CI
steps.description: Use -detailed-exitcode to programmatically determine if changes are pending (exit 2) or clean (exit 0).
steps.tip: Exit code 0 means no changes, 1 means error, 2 means changes pending. Useful for CI/CD decision logic.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Safe Terraform Plan-Apply Workflow
# Reviews plan and prompts before applying

echo "==> Running terraform plan..."
terraform plan -out=tfplan -detailed-exitcode 2>&1 | tee /tmp/tf-plan-output.txt
EXIT_CODE=${PIPESTATUS[0]}

if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "No changes needed. Infrastructure is up to date."
  exit 0
elif [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 1 ]; then
  echo "ERROR: Plan failed. Check the output above."
  exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "==> Plan summary:"
terraform show -no-color tfplan | grep -E "^(Plan:|  #|  \+|  -|  ~)" | head -30

echo ""
read -p "Apply this plan? (yes/no): " CONFIRM
if [ "$CONFIRM" = "yes" ]; then
  echo "==> Applying plan..."
  terraform apply tfplan
  echo "==> Apply complete."
else
  echo "==> Apply cancelled."
  rm -f tfplan
fi

faq.question: Why save the plan to a file instead of applying directly?
faq.answer: Between running plan and apply, resources may change. A saved plan guarantees the exact changes you reviewed are applied, with no surprises from concurrent modifications.

faq.question: Is it safe to use -target regularly?
faq.answer: Targeted applies should be used sparingly. They can leave state inconsistent because dependent resources are skipped. Use them for debugging or emergency fixes, not as standard workflow.
